skill.name: frontend-dev
skill.description: Full-stack frontend development combining premium UI design, cinematic animations, AI-generated media assets, persuasive copywriting, and visual art. Builds complete, visually striking web pages with real media, advanced motion, and compelling copy. Use when: building landing pages, marketing sites, product pages, dashboards, generating media assets (image/video/audio/music), writing conversion copy, creating generative art, or implementing cinematic scroll animations.
skill.summary: Builds production-ready frontend pages with AI-generated images, video, music, and text-to-speech, combined with cinematic animations and persuasive copywriting.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 1176 patterns with a risk score of 100/100, but evaluation confirms these are overwhelmingly false positives. High-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings in canvas-fonts/*.txt files are font Open Font License texts, not crypto code. 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings in markdown reference files are backtick-enclosed code examples in documentation. 'Windows SAM database' finding at templates/viewer.html:508 is the word 'CUSTOMIZE' containing the substring 'SAM'. regex.exec() in generator_template.js:133 is a standard JavaScript hex color parser. The skill is a legitimate frontend development tool with MiniMax API client scripts that properly use environment variables for API key management. Low risk after review.

medium_findings.title: API key stored in module-level variable
medium_findings.description: MINIMAX_API_KEY is read from environment at module import time and stored in a module-level variable. While this is standard practice for API clients, the key persists in memory for the process lifetime.

medium_findings.title: File write operations for generated media
medium_findings.description: Scripts write generated media (images, video, audio) to local filesystem. This is expected behavior for a media generation tool. File paths are user-controlled via command line arguments.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm in font license files
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' in canvas-fonts/*-OFL.txt files. These are Open Font License text files for bundled fonts, containing no code. Pattern matching triggered on license boilerplate text.

low_findings.title: False positive: Shell backtick execution in markdown documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in markdown reference files. These are backtick-enclosed code examples and CLI command documentation, not actual shell execution. The files are reference guides for users.

low_findings.title: False positive: Windows SAM database reference
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'Windows SAM database' at templates/viewer.html:508. The actual text is '// PARTICLE SYSTEM - CUSTOMIZE FOR YOUR ALGORITHM'. The substring 'SAM' within 'CUSTOMIZE' triggered a false regex match.

low_findings.title: False positive: Python exec() function
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'Python exec() function' at templates/generator_template.js:133. The actual code is '/^#?([a-f\d]{2})([a-f\d]{2})([a-f\d]{2})$/i.exec(hex)', which is a standard JavaScript regex method for parsing hex color strings.

low_findings.title: False positive: System reconnaissance in font license files
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'system reconnaissance' patterns in OFL license files. These contain URLs to font directories and repository links as part of standard Open Font License attribution requirements.

low_findings.title: False positive: Hardcoded URLs in font license files
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'hardcoded URL' in canvas-fonts/*.txt license files. These are standard Open Font License repository and directory URLs, not actual network requests or data exfiltration.

content.user_title: Build frontend pages with AI media
content.value_statement: Creating professional web pages requires design, media, animations, and copywriting skills. This skill combines all five capabilities into one workflow, generating complete pages with AI-created images, video, music, voiceovers, and scroll animations.
actual_capabilities: Generate complete frontend pages using React, Vue, Svelte, Astro, or pure HTML
Create AI-generated images via MiniMax text-to-image API
Produce AI-generated music and audio via MiniMax music generation API
Generate text-to-speech voiceovers with multiple voice options
Create AI-generated video clips with camera movement controls
Build cinematic scroll animations using Framer Motion and GSAP
limitations: Requires a valid MiniMax API key for media generation features
Media generation is synchronous and may timeout for large outputs
Video generation uses async polling and has longer wait times
Generated media quality depends on MiniMax API model capabilities
Environment variables must be configured before first use

use_cases.title: Marketing landing page creation
use_cases.description: A startup founder needs a landing page with hero images, background video, and persuasive copy. The skill generates the complete page with AI-created media assets.

use_cases.title: Portfolio website with generative art
use_cases.description: A designer wants a portfolio featuring interactive p5.js generative art, smooth page transitions, and custom typography. The skill builds the art system and full site layout.

use_cases.title: Product demo with voiceover and video
use_cases.description: A product manager needs a demo page with AI-narrated tutorials, generated background music, and product showcase images. The skill creates all media and assembles the page.

prompt_templates.title: Basic landing page
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a landing page for a coffee subscription service. Include a hero section, features grid, and pricing cards. Use React with Tailwind CSS.

prompt_templates.title: Page with AI images
prompt_templates.scenario: Add AI-generated images to a page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a travel blog homepage with AI-generated hero images for destinations: 'tropical beach sunset', 'mountain village morning', 'city skyline at night'. Use Next.js with Framer Motion for fade-in animations.

prompt_templates.title: Multimedia marketing site
prompt_templates.scenario: Full page with video, music, and voiceover
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a product launch page with an AI-generated hero video, background music, and a narrated product demo section. Use AI images for feature cards. Include scroll-triggered animations with GSAP. Deploy as pure HTML.

prompt_templates.title: Interactive art installation page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an interactive art gallery page with a p5.js generative art piece as the hero. Include a parameter control panel for users to adjust the art. Add ambient background music generated by AI. Use Vue with cinematic scroll animations.

output_examples.input: Build a landing page for a fitness app with hero image and feature sections
output: Generated: hero image via MiniMax image API
Generated: Framer Motion scroll animations for feature cards
Generated: Tailwind CSS styling with responsive layout

output_examples.input: Create a product page with AI video demo and background music
output: Generated: Next.js page with video hero section
Generated: AI video clip via MiniMax video API with push-in camera movement
Generated: Ambient background music via MiniMax music API
Generated: GSAP timeline for scroll-triggered content reveals

best_practices: Set MINIMAX_API_KEY and MINIMAX_API_BASE environment variables before using media generation features
Use specific, detailed prompts for AI media generation to get higher quality results
Test generated media assets before deploying to production, as AI output quality varies
anti_patterns: Do not commit API keys to version control, always use environment variables
Do not use AI-generated media without reviewing for quality and appropriateness
Avoid generating too many media assets in parallel, which may exceed API rate limits

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. All three AI tools can orchestrate the frontend generation and MiniMax API calls.

faq.question: Do I need a MiniMax API key?
faq.answer: Yes, media generation features (images, video, music, text-to-speech) require a MINIMAX_API_KEY. Set it as an environment variable before use. The frontend design features work without it.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports Pure HTML, React with Next.js, Vue with Nuxt, Svelte with SvelteKit, and Astro. Each framework uses the appropriate asset and component directory structure.

faq.question: How long does media generation take?
faq.answer: Image generation is typically synchronous and completes within seconds. Music and text-to-speech are also synchronous but may take longer. Video generation is async with polling and can take several minutes.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without AI media generation?
faq.answer: Yes. The frontend design, animation, and copywriting features work independently. Only the media generation scripts require the MiniMax API. You can use placeholder images and existing media instead.

faq.question: What voice options are available for text-to-speech?
faq.answer: The skill supports multiple MiniMax voices including male and female options in various styles. Voice IDs and characteristics are documented in the minimax-voice-catalog reference file.
